name: trace-mcp
description: The CV tool that won't lie for you: the full application flow in chat. Receipt-verified CVs, honest fit scores, interview prep packs with real Anki decks, and an Obsidian career vault. Your data never leaves your machine.
long_description: Trace turns your MCP client into an honest career co-pilot covering the whole application flow. Your CV is parsed into a local Truth Base (~/.trace/), an enrich interview surfaces the evidence you under-sell, jobs are scored honestly (apply / stretch / skip), tailored CVs carry per-claim receipts that the server resolves against the Truth Base in code, and each application gets an interview prep pack: company cheat sheet, question bank, a real importable Anki deck (.apkg), and a morning-of briefing script. A built-in tracker keeps statuses, and setup_obsidian turns the whole thing into an Obsidian vault: a Home dashboard and readable Truth Base note, auto-refreshed on every change, so your career memory persists and is browsable outside chat. Fabrications mechanically fail the receipts gate.

tools.name: get_wizard_state
tools.description: Where the user is in the Trace flow and the suggested next step

tools.name: save_truth_base
tools.description: Persist the user's Truth Base (the only ground truth Trace tailors from)

tools.name: get_truth_base
tools.description: Return the saved Truth Base JSON

tools.name: save_job
tools.description: Persist a parsed job description (requirements + hard blockers) for a company

tools.name: get_job
tools.description: Return the parsed job and recorded fit for a company

tools.name: record_fit
tools.description: Persist the honest fit score and apply/stretch/skip verdict for a company

tools.name: validate_receipts
tools.description: The honesty gate: mechanically resolve every CV claim's JSON path against the Truth Base; fabrications fail

tools.name: export_document
tools.description: Save a finished CV or cover letter for a company (only after receipts pass)

tools.name: list_jobs
tools.description: List companies with saved jobs, fits, statuses, and exported documents

tools.name: set_status
tools.description: Track where an application stands: preparing, applied, interview, offer, rejected, withdrawn

tools.name: build_anki
tools.description: Build a real Anki deck (.apkg) from interview prep cards the user can import into Anki

tools.name: setup_obsidian
tools.description: Turn the Trace folder into an Obsidian vault: Home dashboard + readable Truth Base, auto-refreshed persistent career memory

prompts.name: trace_wizard
prompts.description: Start (or resume) the Trace honest-CV wizard
prompts.text: Run the Trace wizard: call get_wizard_state and guide the user to the next step.

prompts.name: parse_cv
prompts.description: Step 1: parse a pasted CV into the Truth Base
prompts.text: Parse the user's CV into a Trace Truth Base without inventing anything, then save it.

prompts.name: enrich
prompts.description: Step 2: interview the user to fill Truth Base gaps (metrics, scope, voice)
prompts.text: Ask 5-8 sharp questions that surface real omitted evidence, then merge truthful answers into the Truth Base.

prompts.name: score_fit
prompts.description: Step 3: parse a job description and score fit honestly (apply/stretch/skip)
prompts.text: Parse the JD, check hard blockers, and score the Truth Base against it honestly; be willing to say skip.

prompts.name: tailor
prompts.description: Step 4: tailor CV + cover letter with server-verified receipts

arguments.name: company
arguments.description: Company name (must have a recorded fit)

prompts.text: Tailor a CV and cover letter grounded only in the Truth Base, validate every claim with validate_receipts, and export.

prompts.name: prep_pack
prompts.description: Step 5: interview prep pack — company cheat sheet, question bank, Anki deck, briefing script

arguments.name: company
arguments.description: Company name (should have a tailored CV)

prompts.text: Build the four-artifact interview prep pack for the company: cheat sheet, question bank grounded in the Truth Base, Anki cards via build_anki, and a read-aloud briefing script.
